title: The Movie Star Ranking: 35 Over 35, Part 1
format: Sean and Amanda open a two-part countdown of the 35 best movie stars over 35. They lay out their rules, cut the retired and the disqualified, and count down from No. 35 to No. 21.

segments.heading: Setting the ground rules
segments.note: what even counts as a movie star in 2021

films.title: The Little Things
films.note: Denzel Washington's HBO Max legal thriller, cited as proof he can still open a movie: widely watched, but 'not very good.'

segments.heading: Retired, ineligible, and disqualified
segments.note: the movie stars who don't make the cut, and why

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Daniel Day-Lewis's last film before stepping away; the hosts are taking him at his word that it's retirement.

films.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
films.note: Gwyneth Paltrow's cameo as Pepper Potts, the one she famously forgot she was in.

films.title: Coldplay: A Head Full of Dreams
films.note: Paltrow appearing as herself in the tour documentary, one of only four film credits she's had since 2017.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Another of Paltrow's brief Pepper Potts appearances.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Paltrow's last credit as of this recording; also has Robert Redford in a brief time-travel cameo.

films.title: The Old Man & the Gun
films.note: Robert Redford's reported final starring role before retiring.

films.title: Welcome to Mooseport
films.note: The last movie Gene Hackman made before stepping away, 'what feels like 300 years ago.'

films.title: Annie
films.note: One of three Cameron Diaz films from 2014, the year she stopped acting.

films.title: Sex Tape
films.note: Cameron Diaz, same farewell year.

films.title: The Other Woman
films.note: Cameron Diaz's last credit before retiring to make sugar-free wine.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: Gal Gadot's recent lead role; she turned 35 since the hosts' 35-under-35 list, so she ages out of eligibility here.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: Carey Mulligan, another recent graduate off the under-35 list.

films.title: The Dig
films.note: Mulligan and Ralph Fiennes; Sean expected 'English Patient 2' until Fiennes's accent set it apart.

films.title: Antebellum
films.note: Janelle Monáe's recent starring role, the third of the newly-turned-35 crop.

films.title: The Midnight Sky
films.note: George Clooney's Netflix film, widely watched mostly because he's in it, which is itself the point about him missing this list.

films.title: Money Monster
films.note: Clooney's previous starring role, and also part of a run of misfires for costar Julia Roberts.

films.title: Hostiles
films.note: Christian Bale, counted here even though the hosts treat its long shelf life as basically a 2016 release.

films.title: Vice
films.note: Bale's Dick Cheney transformation, one of only three lead roles the hosts credit him with in five years.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Bale's most recent starring role, and, per Sean, the film where he outshines the man ranked above him, Matt Damon.

films.title: Ben Is Back
films.note: Julia Roberts and Lucas Hedges; genuinely good, per the hosts, despite thin chemistry.

films.title: Wonder
films.note: Roberts's overlooked box-office hit, based on the R.J. Palacio novel.

films.title: Mirror Mirror
films.note: Cited as the start of a Roberts dip.

films.title: Larry Crowne
films.note: Another entry from that stretch.

films.title: Eat Pray Love
films.note: Same era of Roberts's filmography.

films.title: Mother's Day
films.note: One of several Roberts misfires from the mid-2010s.

films.title: Secret in Their Eyes
films.note: Another from that run.

films.title: August: Osage County
films.note: The last of the Roberts titles cited as evidence she's stepped back from movies.

films.title: Skyfall
films.note: Javier Bardem's Bond villain; Sean is still mad he didn't win an Oscar for it.

films.title: Ash Is Purest White
films.note: Zhao Tao's pick: 'among the best movie performances I've ever seen.'

films.title: Mountains May Depart
films.note: One of three straight Zhao Tao films directed by her husband, Jia Zhangke.

films.title: A Touch of Sin
films.note: The third of that run with Jia Zhangke.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson: the solo film, arriving on top of the rest of her MCU work.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson: one of two Oscar nominations she picked up the same year.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson: her other 2019 nomination, playing well against her MCU type.

films.title: Lost in Translation
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson: a big one for Sean personally.

films.title: Under the Skin
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson.

films.title: Lucy
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson.

films.title: Ghost in the Shell
films.note: No. 21 Scarlett Johansson: the whitewashing controversy, alongside the 'I could play a tree' interview.

films.title: Joker
films.note: No. 22 Joaquin Phoenix: the Oscar win, and, per Tarantino on a recent podcast, a movie that gets you rooting for a genuinely dangerous person.

films.title: Mary Magdalene
films.note: No. 22 Joaquin Phoenix: one of the arthouse side projects that got a little overlooked.

films.title: You Were Never Really Here
films.note: No. 22 Joaquin Phoenix: another of those overlooked ones.

films.title: The Master
films.note: No. 22 Joaquin Phoenix: the kind of event every Phoenix project becomes.

films.title: Her
films.note: No. 22 Joaquin Phoenix.

films.title: Walk the Line
films.note: No. 22 Joaquin Phoenix.

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: written and directed by her husband, John Krasinski.

films.title: A Quiet Place Part II
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: delayed by the pandemic; would've been the signature hit of spring 2020.

films.title: Jungle Cruise
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: a Disney IP tentpole with Dwayne Johnson, directed by Jaume Collet-Serra.

films.title: Edge of Tomorrow
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: her signature part, Rita Vrataski, 'a redefinition of what a hero in a movie is.'

films.title: Sicario
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt.

films.title: Mary Poppins Returns
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: she played Mary Poppins, which the hosts note is a really big deal.

films.title: The Girl on the Train
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: a shaky adaptation of the airport thriller.

films.title: The Shallows
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: Collet-Serra again, this time with Blake Lively.

films.title: The Five-Year Engagement
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt.

films.title: The Adjustment Bureau
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt, 'the movie with the hats.'

films.title: The Devil Wears Prada
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: a famous supporting turn, also on Anne Hathaway's résumé.

films.title: The Huntsman: Winter's War
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt.

films.title: Extraction
films.note: No. 24 Chris Hemsworth: one of the few to turn mainstream Hollywood stardom into a real streaming hit; a sequel is already a lock.

films.title: Ghostbusters
films.note: No. 24 Chris Hemsworth: a misfire commercially, but the hosts thought he was very funny in it.

films.title: Deadpool
films.note: No. 25 Ryan Reynolds: the franchise he spent a decade trying to find.

films.title: Pokémon Detective Pikachu
films.note: No. 25 Ryan Reynolds: 'a very sweet homage to Humphrey Bogart movies' that is also definitely a Pokémon movie.

films.title: The Hitman's Bodyguard
films.note: No. 25 Ryan Reynolds: a $200 million hidden hit alongside Samuel L. Jackson, Gary Oldman, and Salma Hayek.

films.title: Spectre
films.note: No. 26 Daniel Craig: the last Bond film before this one, six years back by the time of this recording.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: No. 26 Daniel Craig: delayed repeatedly; the hosts have sworn off getting their hopes up again.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: No. 26 Daniel Craig: the post-Bond direction, fun and loose, a hint of what he wants to do next.

films.title: Logan Lucky
films.note: No. 26 Daniel Craig.

films.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
films.note: No. 26 Daniel Craig.

films.title: Kings
films.note: No. 26 Daniel Craig: with Halle Berry, a film Sean admits he'd never heard of.

films.title: Creed
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson.

films.title: Creed II
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson: the smaller, riskier project that balances out her franchise work.

films.title: Annihilation
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson.

films.title: Selma
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson.

films.title: Dear White People
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson.

films.title: Sylvie's Love
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson.

films.title: Men in Black: International
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson: not good, but she tried.

films.title: Passing
films.note: No. 27 Tessa Thompson: seen at Sundance, headed to Netflix later in the year.

films.title: Inside Llewyn Davis
films.note: No. 28 Oscar Isaac.

films.title: Ex Machina
films.note: No. 28 Oscar Isaac.

films.title: Triple Frontier
films.note: No. 28 Oscar Isaac.

films.title: Dune
films.note: No. 28 Oscar Isaac: Sean's most anticipated movie of the year, even without fully knowing what it's about.

films.title: The Card Counter
films.note: No. 28 Oscar Isaac: a Paul Schrader film, Sean's second-most-anticipated of the year.

films.title: Armageddon Time
films.note: No. 28 Oscar Isaac: another James Gray film on the slate, shared with Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Hillbilly Elegy
films.note: No. 29 Amy Adams: roundly rejected critically, though she picked up a SAG nomination for it.

films.title: Vice
films.note: No. 29 Amy Adams.

films.title: Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: No. 29 Amy Adams: the Snyder Cut, which the hosts joke she never asked for.

films.title: The Woman in the Window
films.note: No. 29 Amy Adams: the delayed Joe Wright thriller that's gone through extensive reshoots.

films.title: 12 Years a Slave
films.note: No. 30 Lupita Nyong'o: the Oscar win.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: No. 30 Lupita Nyong'o: the blockbuster check.

films.title: Us
films.note: No. 30 Lupita Nyong'o: a dual performance the hosts think would've earned an Oscar nomination with a fall release date instead of February.

films.title: The 355
films.note: No. 30 Lupita Nyong'o: Simon Kinberg's all-female spy ensemble, with Jessica Chastain.

films.title: Okja
films.note: No. 31 Steven Yeun: Bong Joon Ho, one of the auteur credits that set him apart.

films.title: Burning
films.note: No. 31 Steven Yeun: Lee Chang-dong's film, one of 'the greatest performances in movies in the last couple of years,' per Sean.

films.title: Minari
films.note: No. 31 Steven Yeun: a Best Actor contender, discussed alongside Mahershala Ali as a fellow TV-to-film crossover.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: No. 31 Steven Yeun.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: No. 32 Mahershala Ali: 'mediocre shit,' per Amanda, but one of his two Oscars in three years.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: a very good movie, though Sean thinks Christian Bale outshines him in it.

films.title: Deadpool 2
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: cameo-era Damon.

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: another cameo.

films.title: Unsane
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon.

films.title: Downsizing
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: an Alexander Payne movie Sean likes that most people think is bad.

films.title: Suburbicon
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: a George Clooney film most people, including the hosts, think is bad.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: due in 2020, delayed but reportedly close to finished.

films.title: Stillwater
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: the Tom McCarthy film, also delayed from 2020.

films.title: No Sudden Move
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: a Steven Soderbergh film next on his slate.

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon.

films.title: The Great Wall
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: cited sarcastically as evidence of a spotty stretch.

films.title: The Monuments Men
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: same stretch.

films.title: The Zero Theorem
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: same stretch.

films.title: We Bought a Zoo
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: Amanda's self-professed favorite of the bunch.

films.title: Hereafter
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon.

films.title: Invictus
films.note: No. 33 Matt Damon: the oldest title in a 12-year span of credits Sean rattles off.

films.title: The Dark Knight Rises
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Interstellar
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Les Misérables
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway: the Oscar win.

films.title: The Princess Diaries
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway, with a very young Julie Andrews as costar.

films.title: Rachel Getting Married
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: The Intern
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Ocean's Eight
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway: 'phenomenally funny' in a movie that isn't very good.

films.title: Serenity
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway: part of a rough 2018-2020 run of credits.

films.title: The Hustle
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Dark Waters
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway: a genuinely good film, per Sean, where she's operating on a different level than her costars.

films.title: The Last Thing He Wanted
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Roald Dahl's The Witches
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway.

films.title: Locked Down
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway: her most recent credit.

films.title: Armageddon Time
films.note: No. 34 Anne Hathaway: a James Gray film, and a good sign for what's next.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: No. 35 Chris Evans: the sardonic-asshole mode the hosts think should be his lane going forward.

films.title: Lightyear
films.note: No. 35 Chris Evans: an upcoming animated film inspired by the toy Buzz Lightyear was based on, Evans's own joke tweet made real.

Ads: Netflix's FIFA World Cup Launch Edition
